speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: How now, friend Eros?

speaker: EROS
text: There’s strange news come, sir.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: What, man?

speaker: EROS
text: Caesar and Lepidus have made wars upon

speaker: EROS
text: Pompey.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: This is old. What is the success?

speaker: EROS
text: Caesar, having made use of him in the wars

speaker: EROS
text: ’gainst Pompey, presently denied him rivality,

speaker: EROS
text: would not let him partake in the glory of the action;

speaker: EROS
text: and, not resting here, accuses him of letters he had

speaker: EROS
text: formerly wrote to Pompey; upon his own appeal

speaker: EROS
text: seizes him. So the poor third is up, till death enlarge

speaker: EROS
text: his confine.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Then, world, thou hast a pair of chaps, no more,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And throw between them all the food thou hast,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: They’ll grind the one the other. Where’s Antony?

speaker: EROS
text: He’s walking in the garden, thus, and spurns

speaker: EROS
text: The rush that lies before him; cries “Fool Lepidus!”

speaker: EROS
text: And threats the throat of that his officer

speaker: EROS
text: That murdered Pompey.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Our great navy’s rigged.

speaker: EROS
text: For Italy and Caesar. More, Domitius:

speaker: EROS
text: My lord desires you presently. My news

speaker: EROS
text: I might have told hereafter.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: ’Twill be naught,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But let it be. Bring me to Antony.

speaker: EROS
text: Come, sir.
